name: Anthropic API — compaction and context editing
context_primitives: Two beta APIs: (1) **Compaction** (header compact-2026-01-12): when input tokens exceed a trigger, the server **summarizes** older turns into a compaction block and **drops** prior messages on subsequent requests—explicitly for long chats and tool-heavy agents. (2) **Context editing** (header context-management-2025-06-27): **clear_tool_uses** replaces old tool results with placeholders; **clear_thinking** trims extended-thinking blocks. Plus **prompt caching** for stable prefixes. This is **platform-managed** lossy compression vs. application-owned head/tail stores.
deployment_pattern: Enable context_management.edits in Messages API; choose compaction trigger vs. selective tool clearing; cache system prompts and tool schemas.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: N/A as product feature; Anthropic documents use cases (long sessions, agentic tool loops) rather than publishing Alex-style long-turn evals.
ecosystem_integrations: Claude apps, Claude Code, Bedrock/Vertex, third-party hosts calling Messages API.
differentiators: Compared to **Arize Alex**: Anthropic uses **server-side summarization** for compaction (closer to what DeLucia **rejected** as primary strategy) but pairs it with **deterministic tool-result clearing**—Alex instead uses **fixed head/tail heuristics + external DB retrieval** and rejected blind summarization for salience control.
significance_for_builders: If you are on Claude, you can adopt **official compaction** instead of custom middle truncation—but still add **evals at turn N+1** (Alex lesson) because any lossy policy can fail late.
key_timeline_or_milestones: Compaction beta compact-2026-01-12; context editing beta 2025-06-27; supported on Opus 4.6/4.7, Sonnet 4.6, Mythos preview per docs.
